questions.question: What is the Model Context Protocol (MCP)?
options: An open standard that connects Claude to external tools and services
A proprietary Anthropic API for database access
A programming language for writing Claude extensions
A security protocol for encrypting Claude conversations
questions.explanation: MCP is an open standard that extends Claude's capabilities by connecting it to external tools and services through a client-server architecture.

questions.question: What are the two MCP server transport types?
questions.explanation: MCP supports stdio (local child process communicating via stdin/stdout) and SSE (remote Server-Sent Events over HTTP) transport types.

questions.question: Where are MCP servers configured?
options: In CLAUDE.md at the project root
questions.explanation: MCP servers are configured in settings.json files, either globally (~/.claude/settings.json) or at the project level (.claude/settings.json).

questions.question: How do MCP tools initially appear in a conversation?
options: They are fully loaded with complete schemas from the start
They appear as deferred tools with only their names visible
They must be manually imported with a special command
They are listed in CLAUDE.md and parsed at startup
questions.explanation: MCP tools appear as deferred tools in <available-deferred-tools> messages. Only their names are visible until their full schemas are fetched with ToolSearch.

questions.question: What tool is used to fetch the full schema of an MCP tool before calling it?
questions.explanation: ToolSearch fetches complete JSON Schema definitions for deferred tools. Use 'select:ToolName' for exact matches or keywords for discovery.

questions.question: In the MCP architecture, what role does Claude Code play?
questions.explanation: Claude Code acts as the MCP Client, sending requests to MCP servers which in turn communicate with external services.
